refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.

potential_sources: Interaction between adaptive bitrate control and teleoperation command latency.
Dependency on network link quality and variability.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the FR with minimal coupling to other system functions; error correction and bitrate adaptation are localized solutions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Risk of insufficient error correction under extreme network degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating fallback mechanisms or redundant channels if adaptive bitrate alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware modules responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics including packet loss.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope; contains teleoperation but also other functions, leading to moderate containment and higher diffusion.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control; low containment for communication quality management.
High containment score as communication quality management is core to teleoperation; low diffusion risk.
Safety Services may consume communication quality data but does not manage teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of cross-module coordination if safety alerts depend on communication quality.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Safety Services and NGHE Solution for communication quality data sharing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential interface dependencies with Network Link and Telemetry are well-defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate, reducing diffusion and improving modularity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface and testing under network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or redundancy DP options if adaptive bitrate proves insufficient.
Clarify interface contracts with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to a well-contained IM with clear interfaces and ownership. Verification and risk considerations are addressed adequately.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system modules.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; recommended revisions are for future consideration.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms can meet packet loss targets.
open_risks: Extreme network degradation beyond adaptive algorithm capabilities.
evidence_needed: Performance data of adaptive bitrate and error correction under representative network conditions.
Interface stability and reliability tests for Network Link and Telemetry.
